message:
test(goldens): refresh introspect + schema goldens to advertise rebuild_progress

Commit f22d4a43 ("fix(introspect): advertise rebuild progress
schemas") taught the introspect/robot contract layer about the
rebuild_progress field emitted by status and health, but the
matching golden snapshots weren't regenerated in the same commit,
so the golden suite was left in a state where the introspect
output *did* advertise rebuild_progress on disk but the pinned
goldens didn't. Every robot/golden test run would show the
introspect diff even on a clean tree.

This commit regenerates those three goldens to line up with the
post-f22d4a43 output:

- tests/golden/robot/introspect.json.golden (+357 lines): adds
  the rebuild_progress property object under both the
  status-response schema and the health-response schema. The
  property set mirrors the struct definition: active (boolean),
  runtime_available (boolean), and 15 nullable-integer / nullable-
  string / nullable-number fields — mode, phase,
  processed_conversations, total_conversations,
  remaining_conversations, completion_ratio, indexed_docs,
  queue_depth, queue_capacity, queue_headroom,
  pending_batch_conversations, pending_batch_message_bytes,
  max_message_bytes_in_flight, inflight_message_bytes,
  inflight_message_bytes_headroom, controller_mode,
  controller_reason, updated_at, started_at. Each nullable field
  gets a `["string","null"]` / `["integer","null"]` /
  `["number","null"]` type array so schema consumers know to
  expect nulls when rebuild is inactive.
- tests/golden/robot/introspect_shape.json.golden (+702 lines):
  the "shape of the shape" golden — the same properties, but
  wrapped in the outer `properties/type/properties` skeleton
  used by the shape-conformance test. Both status and health
  response shapes now expose rebuild_progress as a nested
  object-of-typed-properties.
- tests/golden/robot_docs/schemas.txt.golden (+63 lines): the
  human-readable schemas.txt snapshot grows a `rebuild_progress:
  object` block under status and health, listing every property
  with either its concrete type (boolean) or `?` for the
  nullable/union types. This is what `cass schemas` prints.

Test-only change — no production code touched. Regenerate command
recorded in each test file's header still works (UPDATE_GOLDENS=1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
